blockingIssues.description: Webhook delivery-key extraction only honors `X-Gitlab-Delivery` and legacy `X-Gitlab-Event-UUID`; on the mission's GitLab 16.4+ target, deliveries sent with `X-Gitlab-Webhook-UUID` fall back to synthetic keys, breaking reliable deduplication and audit correlation.

The runtime ingress path never emits `hook_source=group` for real group merge-request webhooks because non-system hooks default to `project`, so production behavior does not match the feature's group/project/system normalization contract.

Deferred head-SHA handling is only kept in memory: missing-SHA events persist neither the deferred intent nor a later lookup path, so distinct missing-SHA triggers can collapse onto the same synthetic idempotency key instead of being resolved later.

Cancelling a running review run only flips database state; the scheduler still finishes `ProcessRun` and unconditionally writes `completed` or `failed`, so cancelled runs can continue into downstream work and overwrite the cancelled terminal state.

Retry-scheduled runs are represented as `status='failed'` plus a due `next_retry_at`, but MR close/merge cancellation only targets `pending` and `running` rows, allowing retryable runs to be reclaimed and processed after the MR is closed or merged.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `integrations.md` with the GitLab webhook delivery headers to honor for self-managed GitLab 16.4+ (`X-Gitlab-Delivery`, `X-Gitlab-Webhook-UUID`, legacy `X-Gitlab-Event-UUID`) and noted that project/group MR hooks share the same event header.
Updated `architecture.md` to record that retry-scheduled runs remain active work when stored as `failed` plus `next_retry_at`, so lifecycle/cancellation code must treat them like pending work.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how strictly workers must follow the skill's test-first requirement and require `skillFeedback` to call out transcript-visible deviations when implementation files are created before failing tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature `ingress-normalization-and-triggers` created `internal/hooks/normalize.go` before `internal/hooks/normalize_test.go` in the transcript skeleton while still reporting `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, which makes scrutiny harder to evaluate consistently.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note that adapter/handler integration tests may need an external `*_test` package when importing a sibling package would otherwise create an import cycle.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature `ingress-run-lifecycle` had to move lifecycle handler coverage into `internal/hooks/handler_lifecycle_integration_test.go` with `package hooks_test` because importing `internal/runs` from the in-package handler tests caused an import cycle.
